description: A community-scoped question or proposal under collective consideration. Companion records `dev.idiolect.deliberationStatement` carry participant utterances, `dev.idiolect.deliberationVote` carries stances on those statements, and `dev.idiolect.deliberationOutcome` carries the observer-aggregated tally. Deliberations are intentionally process-shaped: they represent the *unsettled* moment, distinct from `dev.idiolect.belief` (settled doxastic) and `dev.idiolect.recommendation` (settled normative). A deliberation that closes can name an outcome record so consumers can read the conclusion without re-folding the votes.

main.description: A deliberation: a topic or question owned by a community, optionally bracketed by a status and classification. Topic and description carry the human framing; classification and status are open-enum slugs resolved through community-published vocabularies, so a community can decline to take a stance on those axes simply by omitting them.

owningCommunity.description: AT-URI of the `dev.idiolect.community` record whose membership is deliberating. Consumers resolve membership/permissions through that record.

description.description: Extended framing or context for the deliberation.

authRequired.description: Whether participation requires the participant be an authenticated member of `owningCommunity`. False permits drive-by statements.

classification.description: Open-enum slug naming the deliberation's shape. Resolved against `classificationVocab` when present, otherwise against the canonical idiolect vocabulary.

classificationVocab.description: Vocabulary the `classification` slug resolves against. Omit to use the canonical idiolect default.

status.description: Open-enum lifecycle marker. Resolved against `statusVocab` when present.

closedAt.description: When the deliberation moved out of an open status. Optional; absent means open or never explicitly closed.

outcome.description: AT-URI of a `dev.idiolect.deliberationOutcome` record summarising the resolved stance. Set after closure when an outcome exists; consumers can read it without re-folding the vote stream.
